title: viola palustris var. pyrenaica
intro: Viola palustris var. pyrenaica, also known as Pyrenean marsh violet, is a subspecies of the marsh violet plant that belongs to the violet family, Violaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Viola palustris var. pyrenaica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as an antiseptic. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
